Writing: Hoping this year is a slam dunk! The Axelotls worked hard to prepare a flower stand featuring an array of bright blooms that positively erupt with passion and charm, just like their oshi! Dotted with bright oranges and yellows, these flowers are bursting with the kind of persistence and strength that our resident hitman, doctor, assassin, gladiator idol inspires in us. Don't mind the scratches. Do you know how hard it is to climb with tiny arms?

With a dazzling array of blue and orange flowers carefully arranged, this flower stand is dedicated to both Shiny and Spicy. A variety of blooms are displayed; from bright Gerbera Daisies, Marigolds, Forget-Me-Nots, Cosmos, Hydrangeas, and Carnations, to intensely-hued Irises, Hyacinths, Bluebells, and Lilies. There's even a few Birds of Paradise! Many stars shine from behind and within the flowers to support the beloved guiding lights that make up Tempus HQ, Altare and Axel.

The Altventurers have prepared a flower stand adorned with various flowers! Symbolizing their utmost gratitude and the strength to carry on through whatever challenges you might face, this flower stand contains only the purest form of love and gratitude for their beloved oshi. May the stars guide you through your journey, which, although challenging, shall be filled with love and warmth.

Entry Title: Demon King and Fire Prince Learn The Proper Way To Rule
Writing: Demon King and Fire Prince travel to Elysiamexico and learn how to rule from the Zapotec Civilization at Monte Alban. This civilization is approximately 2500 years old, since 500 BCE. It served as a major political and economic capital. Only royalty and priests were able to set foot in these temples excluding the peasants far from the site. Near the center of the area is where rituals and sacrifice were performed for the gods. Something the Demon King and Fire Prince took an interest in. >:D

Entry Title: Demon King and Fire Prince Learn How To Eat Like True Royalty
Writing: Demon King and Fire Prince try the traditional dish tlayudas. Often referred to as "Oaxacan pizza," this dish starts with a massive, thin corn tortilla toasted over an open flame. It is spread with asiento (pork lard), refried black beans, cabbage, Oaxacan string cheese, and usually topped with meats like tasajo (thin-sliced cured beef) or cecina (seasoned pork).
